summary: Excellent matrix recording of the March‑26‑1988 Hampton show – crisp, rich sound captures a high‑energy set highlighted by Watchtower, Peggy‑O and a soaring China > Rider > Playin’ sequence.
review: The Braverman matrix tape delivers crystal‑clear instrumentation with a warm blend of board and audience ambience. The band is tight and exuberant, delivering standout moments on Watchtower, Peggy‑O, Memphis Blues and an extended China > Rider > Playin’/UJB jam, while the notorious Stir It Up runs a mixed bag. Overall the performance and recording are both top‑tier for this era.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews, the vast majority rate 4–5 stars and praise both the musical performance and the sound quality; only a handful of minor criticisms (Stir It Up, track‑title errors) keep the rating just below flawless.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Rich matrix mix from Braverman – crisp highs, solid mids and clean bass. Reviewers repeatedly call it “fantastic”, “great mix” and “crisp”. Only occasional comment about a “brutal” Stir It Up segment, but no major drop‑outs or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls with high‑energy numbers (Sugaree, Memphis Blues) and tight playing; the second set launches into a monster jam on China > Rider > Playin' that flows seamlessly into UJB and drums, delivering the show’s emotional peak.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for clean tone, explosive lead on I Know You Rider, strong vocal delivery (though one note of reedy voice).
band_member_comments.Phil: Positive remarks about his bass foundation in space and overall tightness; no specific criticisms.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for clean guitar tone and competitive interplay with Jerry during Memphis Blues; generally praised.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland highlighted for solid work in space and supportive comping throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both percussionists described as mesmerizing, with “drumz” receiving particular kudos during the extended jams.
